steps.title: Use the stated goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize two things at once: duration, meaning how long Carter and Silva have been doing this work, and purpose, meaning what the work is meant to accomplish. The relevant notes say both women founded Gullah museums in 2003 and both are preservationists connected to Gullah culture.

steps.title: Keep only relevant details
steps.content:
• Choice A - At the Gullah Museums in Hilton Head Island and Georgetown, South Carolina, visitors can learn more about the Gullah people who have lived in the region for centuries.: this focuses on visitors, not the duration or purpose of Carter's and Silva's work.
  • Choice B - Maria Ellis Carter and Ana Silva have worked to preserve the culture of the Gullah people, who have lived in the United States since the 18th century.: this gives the purpose but not the duration of their work.

steps.title: Select both required points
steps.content:
• Choice C - Since 2003, Maria Ellis Carter and Ana Silva have worked to preserve Gullah culture through their museums.: "Since 2003" gives duration, and "preserve Gullah culture" gives purpose.
  • Choice D - Influenced by the traditions of West and Central Africa, Gullah culture developed in parts of the southeastern United States in the 18th century.: this describes Gullah culture, not the two historians' work.
The answer is C.
